articles.title: How to apply to Startup Battlefield 2026, what you need ahead of the June 8 deadline
articles.description: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

articles.title: The internet is being rebuilt for machines
articles.description: As AI agents move from experiments to production, AWS, Cloudflare, and others are redesigning cloud infrastructure for a future dominated by machine-generated internet traffic instead of human users.

articles.title: Slate Auto will announce pricing and take preorders for its EV on June 24
articles.description: The Bezos-backed EV startup has yet to announce final pricing for its vehicle, which is supposed to start shipping by the end of this year.

articles.title: Asana acquires no-code agent-builder StackAI
articles.description: Asana will incorporate StackAI into its growing suite of AI workflow tools.

articles.title: Bluesky embraces long-form content to counter X Articles
articles.description: In its latest update, Bluesky is getting into long-form content.

articles.title: Hackers are trying to steal Signal users’ backups in new wave of phishing attacks
articles.description: A new hacking campaign is trying to trick Signal users to give up their secret recovery key, which can be used to access online backups containing past messages.

articles.title: Anthropic raises $65 billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO
articles.description: Anthropic has closed a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, marking what could be the AI startup's final private fundraise before a highly anticipated IPO.

articles.title: Just like gold and oil, we’ll soon be able to trade AI token futures
articles.description: Large exchanges are designing derivative products around AI tokens, which are increasingly being considered less a computational output and more a raw material input, like electricity or bandwidth.

articles.title: H1 secures $40M from CVS, proving SaaS startups can still attract investment
articles.description: CEO Ariel Katz argues that while AI can replicate workflow SaaS, it can't copy H1’s unique doctor data.

articles.title: A security lapse at prison pay phone service Pay Tel publicly exposed over 300K callers’ driver’s licenses
articles.description: Pay Tel secured the publicly exposed data after security researchers discovered the leak containing callers' sensitive ID documents and inmate communications.

articles.title: Why Paris may be the most important AI city outside Silicon Valley
articles.description: Europe’s startup ecosystem has matured significantly; its founders are increasingly willing to scale companies domestically instead of immediately looking to relocate to the U.S.

articles.title: In just 3 weeks, StrictlyVC is coming to Los Angeles
articles.description: StrictlyVC Los Angeles is on June 18. Join for meaningful networking and fireside chats with leaders from Mach Industries, Shinkei Systems, and more. Register today.

articles.title: Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline extended to June 8 after overwhelming demand
articles.description: After overwhelming demand from founders around the world, TechCrunch has extended the Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline to June 8. Nominate a standout startup or apply yours today.

articles.title: Corgi announces $106M raise at $2.6B valuation — double what it was worth 3 weeks ago
articles.description: While startups raising back-to-back rounds at steep step-ups have become almost routine, a company whose valuation doubles in three weeks is unusual enough to raise questions, particularly given the investor set in both rounds is the same.

articles.title: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new ‘dynamic workflow’ tool
articles.description: The new Opus model comes with a tool called Dynamic Workflows, for coordinating swarms of subagents.

articles.title: Waymo’s newest robotaxi is Chinese-made, built to make money, and now accepting riders
articles.description: The launch of the Ojai minivan robotaxi comes after years of development and testing, but arrives amid a challenging time for Waymo.

articles.title: US says troops were targeted with location data, as senator warns ad industry is a ‘national security threat’
articles.description: One leading privacy lawmaker said it was time to "start treating the adtech industry as a national security threat."

articles.title: How long is Anthropic’s lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary
articles.description: Elon Musk is publicly reframing xAI’s massive Anthropic compute deal as short-term and cancellable, despite SpaceX’s own S-1 filing describing payments through May 2029.

articles.title: Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app
articles.description: Sesame’s new iOS app brings its conversational AI agents to the public, offering more natural back-and-forth interactions designed to feel less like traditional chatbots and more like talking to a person.

articles.title: Sneak peek at new Siri app reveals Apple’s plans to take on ChatGPT and more
articles.description: New renders offer a closer look at Apple’s planned AI overhaul for iOS 27, including a redesigned Siri experience and standalone Siri app.

articles.title: 2027 Audi RS5 first drive: A performance PHEV with split personalities
articles.description: Audi has developed an entirely new electric torque-vectoring rear differential.

articles.title: LLMs believe false statements even after explicit warnings that they're false
articles.description: Fine-tuning tests show "bias ... toward confidently representing the claims as true."

articles.title: Fed up with vibe coders, dev sneaks data-nuking prompt injection into their code
articles.description: Undisclosed addition in jqwik instructed AI coding agents to delete app output.

articles.title: US healthcare still stupidly expensive, with pathetic outcomes, study finds
articles.description: There are strategies to improve healthcare, but US isn't trying them.

articles.title: Researchers develop a new process to get lithium out of rocks
articles.description: If it scales up, it can help us diversify our sources of a key element.

articles.title: FBI says Google engineer used internal search data to win $1.2M on Polymarket
articles.description: FBI: Googler who knew outcome of bets in advance made $1.2M profit on Polymarket.

articles.title: A respectable port of Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition invades macOS
articles.description: The port seems solid, and all DLC is supported—but there's no crossplay, sadly.

articles.title: Apple working to cram massive Gemini model into iPhone to power new Siri

articles.title: Amazon turns to Jeff Bezos' other company to do some heavy lifting
articles.description: Amazon is turning a corner with its launch providers, but ULA's Vulcan remains grounded.

articles.title: How pigeons exploit magnetic fields for navigation

articles.title: Intel makes a bid for handheld gaming PCs with new Arc G3 processors
articles.description: Intel's Arc B390 integrated GPU has offered impressive performance in laptops.

articles.title: Trump loses more control over AI regulation as Illinois passes landmark law

articles.title: Steam Deck sells out in North America within 24 hours of price hike

articles.title: Bad cholesterol slashed 62% by single dose of gene-editing drug in small trial

articles.title: Forecasters predict below-average hurricane season, advise against complacency

articles.title: California defeats Tesla's attempt to throw out racial discrimination lawsuit
articles.description: California civil rights agency hails win over Tesla, anticipates trial in July.

articles.title: Websites have a new way to spy on visitors: Analyzing their SSD activity
articles.description: Telltale SSD activity can be measured in the browser using simple JavaScript.

articles.title: Mystery GPS jammer in Iran becomes test for NASA satellites’ capabilities
articles.description: NASA science satellites show dual use in locating sources of GPS interference.

articles.title: Mina the Hollower is the best old-school action adventure I've played in a while

articles.title: Nvidia bets $150B on Taiwan as Trump's plan to make US an AI hub backfires
articles.description: Nvidia will invest $150 billion a year to make Taiwan an AI “epicenter.”

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI与资本动态**：Anthropic完成65亿美元融资，估值逼近1万亿美元，并发布Opus 4.8模型及“动态工作流”工具；AI代币期货即将像黄金和石油一样可交易。
2. **创业与投资**：Startup Battlefield 2026申请截止日延长至6月8日；Slate Auto将于6月24日公布电动汽车定价并开启预购；Asana收购无代码智能体构建平台StackAI；H1获CVS 4000万美元投资，显示SaaS初创公司仍具吸引力；Corgi以26亿美元估值完成1.06亿美元融资，三周内估值翻倍。
3. **安全与隐私**：黑客发起新一轮钓鱼攻击，试图窃取Signal用户备份数据；监狱付费电话服务Pay Tel因安全漏洞泄露超30万呼叫者的驾照信息。
4. **平台与内容演变**：Bluesky引入长文内容功能以对抗X平台的文章模式；互联网正被重构为机器服务的新基础设施。
5. **区域与活动**：巴黎被认为可能是硅谷以外最重要的AI城市；StrictlyVC活动将于三周后在洛杉矶举办。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：Anthropic以近万亿美元估值完成65亿美元融资并发布新一代模型，标志着AI领域的资本与技术创新竞争进入新阶段。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **AI Agent性能基准与工程实践**：ITBench-AA首次建立企业IT任务Agent基准，前沿模型得分不足50%；同时业界涌现多项Agent相关进展，如Cisco与OpenAI用Codex重新定义企业工程、OpenAI推出自改进税务Agent，以及Harness、Scaffold等Agent核心术语的规范化讨论。

2. **模型创新与部署新趋势**：Nemotron-Labs推出接近光速的扩散语言模型文本生成；TRL通过Hub Bucket实现万亿参数模型增量权重同步；Reachy Mini机器人实现完全本地化运行，无需云端依赖。

3. **战略与治理方向突破**：OpenAI发布前沿治理框架，并公布2026年选举信息与保障措施；业界提出“专业化优于规模”的AI采购新策略，强调垂直领域定制化价值；Google I/O 2026发布12项重大更新及Dialogues舞台内容。

4. **企业合作与开源生态**：Warp押注GPT-5.5开源构建；OpenAI与巴西Grupo Folha、Grupo UOL达成战略内容合作；Gartner将OpenAI列为企业编码Agent领导者。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI Agent在企业级任务中的表现仍存显著瓶颈（基准测试低于50%），但专业化、本地化、开源化与治理框架的同步推进，正为下一阶段实用化突破奠定基础。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中国深化社会保障改革**：取消农民工落户限制，推动社会保险体系整合，提升流动人口权益保障。  
2. **科技自主突破与竞争**：华为展示芯片设计新进展，试图突破美国制裁；中国通过旅行限制保留顶尖AI人才，并推进AI代币期货市场建设。  
3. **清洁能源与环保进展**：太阳能扶贫项目降低污染并提高收入；全球最大浮式风力涡轮机完成深海测试。  
4. **国际产业与安全博弈**：全球车企面临中国竞争压力；荷兰官员称中国或对出口公交车启用“远程控制开关”；欧洲政客警告对华政策存在致命错误。  
5. **监控与空间技术争议**：中国升级全球最大监控网络并融入AI技术；分析人士指中国火箭残骸问题持续恶化。  

今日最值得关注的发展：中国在社会保障、AI人才管控及新能源技术上的多线推进，凸显其加速“内循环”与科技自主的战略重心，同时国际社会对华技术依赖与安全担忧同步上升。
